acceptance_targets: [chapter_06_craft_001] PASS if a close read of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:15 no longer identifies Lena's reserve as completed trailer-sale proceeds and no longer implies the Wildwood trailer has already been sold before the later cleanout/sale chapters. FAIL if the revised line still gives her the full trailer-sale cash in hand.

[chapter_06_prose_001] PASS if the revised chapter uses the 'Adults ...' construction no more than twice across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:13, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:397, and the closing shift is legible through concrete scene detail rather than a three-sentence aphoristic stack. FAIL if the ending still resolves Lena's thinking through three consecutive 'Adults ...' declarations or an equivalent thematic chant.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a close read of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:15 no longer identifies Lena's reserve as completed trailer-sale proceeds and no longer implies the Wildwood trailer has already been sold before the later cleanout/sale chapters. FAIL if the revised line still gives her the full trailer-sale cash in hand.
findings.problem: The chapter says Lena already has the full $18,600 in 'trailer-sale money' sitting in savings. The continuity sheet makes the Wildwood trailer sale a later-story event that closes in chapter_17, so this line breaks the financial timeline and the trailer's object state.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:15 so Lena's protected reserve comes from a source that canonically exists by chapter_06, or recast the line so it no longer implies the Wildwood trailer has already been sold. Preserve the checking-account hit from the $350 transfer, but remove the completed-sale state from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:15.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter uses the 'Adults ...' construction no more than twice across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:13, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:397, and the closing shift is legible through concrete scene detail rather than a three-sentence aphoristic stack. FAIL if the ending still resolves Lena's thinking through three consecutive 'Adults ...' declarations or an equivalent thematic chant.
findings.problem: The chapter leans on the same sententious 'Adults ...' sentence shape five times. What starts as Lena's defensive self-talk becomes an over-literal thematic device in the closing triad, flattening the final turn by explaining the meaning instead of letting the concrete money/work detail and Eli's note carry it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the mantra at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:13, and especially snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:397 so the ending lands through Lena's embodied rationalization rather than three consecutive aphorisms. Keep the callback only if it stays sharp, and let at least part of the justification emerge from the receipt, groceries, work schedule, or Lena's bodily response in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:397.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
